en.extract:
The white-headed bulbul (Hypsipetes thompsoni) is a songbird species in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae.
It is found in Myanmar and north-western Thailand. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest. It is not considered a threatened species by the IUCN.


Taxonomy and systematics
The white-headed bulbul was previously placed in the monotypic genus Cerasophila (literally meaning "cherry-lover"). Following recently molecular phylogenetic studies, it is now placed in the genus Hypsipetes. Alternate names for the white-headed bulbul include Bingham's bulbul and brown-vented bulbul.


Footnotes


References
Gregory, Steven M. (2000): Nomenclature of the Hypsipetes Bulbuls (Pycnonotidae). Forktail 16: 164–166. PDF fulltext

The '''white-headed bulbul''' ('''''Hypsipetes thompsoni''''') is a [[songbird]] [[species]] in the [[bulbul]] [[Family (taxonomy)|family]], Pycnonotidae.<ref name="iucn status 16 November 2021" /><ref name=ioc>{{BioRef|IOC| family=Pycnonotidae|version=Version 10.2 |access-date=3 August 2020}}</ref>

It is found in Myanmar and north-western Thailand. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]. It is not considered a [[threatened species]] by the [[IUCN]].<ref name="iucn status 16 November 2021" />

== Taxonomy and systematics ==

The white-headed bulbul was previously placed in the [[monotypic]] [[genus]] ''Cerasophila'' (literally meaning "[[cherry]]-lover").<ref name="gregory2000">Gregory (2000)</ref> Following recently molecular phylogenetic studies, it is now placed in the genus ''[[Hypsipetes]]''. Alternate names for the white-headed bulbul include '''Bingham's bulbul''' and '''brown-vented bulbul'''.<ref name=ioc/>
